V-M-077.scope: Phase-59 verification for premium Matrix motion and microinteractions across user and admin frontends: copy/download/proxy feedback, VPN/MTProto/subscription/alert/health state transitions, route entrance motion, reduced-motion fallback, inactive-tab lifecycle, keyboard/pointer/scroll safety, and protected-surface isolation.

module-checks.check-9: Chrome screenshot audit is represented by Phase-59 static-route proof marker; authenticated clean-deploy screenshot review remains follow-up.
module-checks.check-10: Interaction smoke is static: copy/download/proxy link classes, route/focus/pointer/reduced-motion markers, passive pointer listener, and no scroll hijack patterns are asserted by Phase-59 smoke.
module-checks.check-11: python3 XML parse for docs/modules/M-077.xml docs/plans/Phase-59.xml docs/verification/V-M-077.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-14: git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example backend/app backend/tests mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy telegram-bot is empty
module-checks.check-15: node scripts/phase59-motion-interactions-smoke.mjs asserts root transform safety, CSS rain fallback, mobile overscan, and visualViewport listeners.
module-checks.check-16: Phase-74 smoke asserted admin nav/logout microinteractions, pointer-safe scroll containers, reduced-motion fallback, and no Matrix overlay scroll interception.
module-checks.check-17: Phase-75 smoke asserted expand/collapse feedback, confirmation pointer safety, reduced-motion fallback, and no Matrix overlay scroll interception in the unified Users inventory.

evidence.item: 2026-06-04: frontend-admin build passed with the existing large chunk warning only.
2026-06-04: Phase-59 motion smoke printed all required MatrixMotion markers.
2026-06-04: Phase-52, Phase-55, Phase-56, Phase-57, and post-commit Phase-58 regression smokes passed.
2026-06-04: XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard passed.
2026-06-04: Rainfix Phase-59 smoke passed after moving motion-route-enter from the root shell to matrix-visual-content and adding CSS_RAIN_FALLBACK_READY/MOBILE_OVERSCAN_READY assertions.
2026-06-04: Phase-68 kept dashboard/config/subscription buttons on existing motion-interactive primitives; Phase-59 and Phase-68 smokes passed with reduced-motion and protected-surface guards.
2026-06-05: Phase-74 smoke passed MatrixMotion markers ADMIN_NAV_MOTION_SAFE and SCROLL_POINTER_SAFE for admin shell navigation and content scrolling.
2026-06-05: Phase-75 smoke passed MatrixMotion markers EXPAND_COLLAPSE_MOTION_SAFE, CONFIRMATION_POINTER_SAFE, and REDUCED_MOTION_EXPANSION_PASS for the unified Users/Devices inventory.

verification-matrix.target-1: Copy, download, QR/config, Telegram proxy, and promo/status feedback use short visible success/error states without blocking forms, buttons, links, or route navigation.
verification-matrix.target-2: VPN, MTProto, subscription, alert, device, server, health, and billing status transitions remain readable while animating and do not resize fixed-format tiles, charts, rows, toolbars, QR blocks, or buttons.
verification-matrix.target-3: Route entrance motion is bounded, fast, and never hides first-screen CTA/action controls or delays keyboard focus restoration.
verification-matrix.target-4: prefers-reduced-motion replaces motion-heavy behavior with static or fade-only variants, disables decorative loop/scanline intensity where needed, and keeps Matrix canvas pointer-safe.
verification-matrix.target-5: Motion CSS and runtime code avoid scroll hijacking, parallax scroll locking, pointer interception, keyboard traps, long cinematic loaders, negative letter spacing, viewport-scaled font sizes, oversized radii, and prohibited decorative orb/radial backgrounds.
verification-matrix.target-6: No backend API, auth/session, deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, MTProto/VPN runtime, telegram-bot, billing catalog, or data model changes are introduced by Phase-59.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: A user copies VPN config data, downloads a config, opens/copies MTProto links, and sees immediate compact feedback that clears without disrupting the workflow.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: An admin reviews devices, MTProto analytics, alerts, plans, servers, and dashboard health while status transitions clarify state without hiding destructive confirmations or redaction boundaries.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Keyboard-only navigation and focus-visible rings remain stable through route transitions and microinteractions.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Reduced-motion mode keeps the product premium through static/fade feedback, not decorative loops or movement-heavy transitions.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Inactive-tab or unmounted-route lifecycle stops animation timers/frames and does not leak work or stale feedback state.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Animation hides operational controls, status text, QR/config content, admin confirmations, MTProto redaction states, tariff prices, or payment/subscription labels.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Canvas, overlay, route wrapper, tooltip, toast, or feedback layer intercepts clicks, focus, copy/download actions, form submission, or mobile scrolling.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Route transitions use long cinematic loaders, block navigation, reset scroll unexpectedly, trap keyboard focus, or delay emergency/admin actions.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Reduced-motion media emulation still runs motion-heavy loops, transform-heavy transitions, flicker, or nonessential animation.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Smoke output, screenshots, or logs reveal MTProto secrets, full proxy links, raw promotion tags, bearer/refresh/reset/verify tokens, real emails, raw IP exports, payment IDs, provider keys, database URLs, or API keys.
failure-scenarios.failure-6: Phase-59 touches backend/deploy/runtime/billing/auth surfaces or changes product behavior outside frontend motion/microinteraction scope.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Allowed smoke output: route names, viewport names, selector names, timing budgets, reduced-motion mode, pointer/scroll/focus pass markers, synthetic fixture IDs, and screenshot paths.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Forbidden smoke output: MTProto ee secrets, full tg:// or https://t.me proxy links, raw promotion tags, bearer/refresh/reset/verify tokens, real user emails, raw IP history exports, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, database URLs, and API keys.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: Any runtime console markers must use stable Phase-59 marker strings and must not include payload values, device identifiers, IP addresses, account emails, or provider metadata.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-077 smoke proves motion budget, microinteraction markers, status transitions, route transitions, reduced-motion fallback, pointer/scroll/focus safety, inactive-tab safety, and protected-surface guard.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-009, M-010, M-070, M-071, M-072, M-075, and M-076 regression smokes remain satisfied after motion changes.
phase-gates.gate-3: User/admin builds, Phase-59 smoke, Phase-52/55/56/57/58 regressions, screenshot or static-route proof, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard pass before Phase-59 can be marked done-local.
phase-gates.gate-4: Route transition animation must remain on the content layer so fixed Matrix canvas/fallback layers are never clipped by a transformed root ancestor.

V-M-077.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for autonomous local execution if changes stay inside user/admin frontend Matrix components, CSS, route wrappers, microinteraction helpers, Phase-59 smoke verification, and MyGRACE docs. Stop if implementation appears to require backend API changes, auth/session changes, deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose changes, MTProto/VPN runtime changes, billing catalog changes, or secret-handling changes.

phase-68-verification-design.scope: Cabinet button and status microinteraction verification.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-1: All user-cabinet buttons must have compact hover, active, focus, loading, and disabled states.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-2: Motion must not hide subscription countdown, tariff price, MTProto secret, QR actions, or referral controls.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-3: prefers-reduced-motion must disable transform-heavy effects while preserving visible feedback.

phase-71-verification-design.scope: Motion verification for QR close icon, selected-device UX, sticky actions, and subscription boundary pulse.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-1: Icon-only close, device row selection, copy/download feedback, and calendar boundary pulse must use bounded motion and visible focus states.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-2: Reduced-motion mode must avoid transform-heavy or flicker-heavy animation while preserving visual meaning.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-3: Motion layers must not intercept pointer, keyboard, copy, download, QR, or mobile scroll actions.

V-M-077.phase-71-evidence: Phase-71 smoke passed for Matrix motion markers, selected-device row feedback, icon-only close, sticky actions, calendar pulse, and reduced-motion safety; post-commit Phase-59 protected regression guard passed cleanly.

phase-72-verification-design.scope: Motion verification for touch/scroll-aware bottom navigation and compact calendar states.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-1: Bottom navigation reveal/compact behavior must use short bounded motion and must not hijack scroll, pointer, keyboard, copy, download, or QR interactions.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-2: Reduced-motion mode must keep navigation reachable without transform-heavy or flicker-heavy transitions.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-3: Calendar density and boundary pulse must remain readable and must not introduce layout shifts during hover, focus, or route transitions.

V-M-077.phase-72-evidence: Phase-72 and Phase-59 smokes passed for bounded bottom-dock transitions, reduced-motion nav safety, pointer/scroll safety, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard.

phase-74-verification-design.scope: Motion and pointer/scroll verification for the admin shell navigation and scroll repair wave.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-1: Logout and admin nav controls may use motion-interactive states but must not shift layout, hide controls, or trap keyboard focus.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-2: Matrix visual layers, route transitions, and feedback layers must not intercept mouse-wheel, touch scroll, pointer events, or focus in the admin content surface.
phase-74-verification-design.gate-3: Reduced-motion mode must keep admin navigation and scroll behavior usable without transform-heavy transitions.

V-M-077.phase-74-evidence: Phase-74 smoke proved admin nav motion and content scroll pointer safety without Matrix overlay scroll interception or protected backend/deploy/runtime drift.

phase-75-verification-design.scope: Motion and pointer/scroll verification for expandable user rows and device confirmations in the unified admin Users inventory.
phase-75-verification-design.gate-1: Expand/collapse interactions must be short, bounded, keyboard reachable, and must not resize unrelated admin shell navigation or headers.
phase-75-verification-design.gate-2: Device action confirmations must remain pointer-safe above Matrix visual layers and must not mutate before explicit confirmation.
phase-75-verification-design.gate-3: Reduced-motion mode must keep expanded device state readable without transform-heavy transitions.
phase-75-verification-design.gate-4: Scroll containers for expanded device lists must not hijack the admin content wheel/touch scroll behavior repaired in Phase-74.

V-M-077.phase-75-evidence: Phase-75 smoke passed expand/collapse, confirmation pointer-safety, and reduced-motion expansion markers without protected backend/deploy/runtime/user frontend drift.

phase-76-verification-design.scope: Motion and microinteraction regression guard across the consolidated user/admin polish gate.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-1: User bottom dock, config actions, subscription calendar, admin nav, content scroll, expanded user rows, and confirmation surfaces must keep bounded motion.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-2: Reduced-motion, pointer/scroll safety, keyboard focus safety, and Matrix overlay non-interception must remain verified by Phase-59 plus Phase-72/74/75 smokes.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-3: Motion execution must not change backend, auth/session, billing, VPN, MTProto, deploy/install, nginx, docker, provider, or telegram-bot surfaces.
phase-76-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase59-motion-interactions-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase74-admin-shell-navigation-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase75-admin-users-devices-smoke.mjs; protected-surface guard.

V-M-077.phase-76-evidence: Consolidated Phase-76 gate passed for Matrix motion: Phase-59 smoke plus Phase-72/74/75 smokes preserved bounded motion, reduced-motion fallback, pointer/scroll safety, keyboard focus safety, bottom dock feedback, admin scroll, and confirmation pointer safety.

phase-77-verification-design.scope: Motion verification for calendar pulses, dock interactions, reduced-motion fallback, and mobile scroll non-interception.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-1: Green/cyan/red calendar pulses must stay bounded and must not create layout shifts.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-2: Reduced-motion must disable or collapse pulse/dock animation while preserving visible state.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-3: Dock, Matrix visual layers, and content wrappers must not hijack pointer, keyboard, copy/download, QR, or mobile scroll interactions.

V-M-077.phase-77-evidence: Phase-59 and Phase-77 smokes passed for bounded calendar pulse classes, fixed dock interaction markers, reduced-motion fallback, pointer-safe Matrix layers, and no mobile scroll hijack.

mobile-route-transform-fix-verification.scope: Mobile-only route transform regression for fixed bottom navigation.
mobile-route-transform-fix-verification.gate-1: Route entrance transform stays available on larger screens but is disabled for mobile user content where it can scope fixed-position descendants.
mobile-route-transform-fix-verification.gate-2: Disabling mobile route transform must not remove reduced-motion policy, copy feedback, status motion, calendar pulses, or pointer-safety markers.
